title: Mr Price Group (JSE: MRP) — Scenario Outlook FY2027 / FY2028
subtitle_lines: Bull / Neutral / Bear projections for the 2026/27 (FY2027) and 2027/28 (FY2028) financial years, with a sentiment read of management and market language.
Internal research note — prepared 18 June 2026. FYE end-March (52/53-week retail calendar). All figures ZAR unless stated.
doc_title: Will the SA cash engine or the NKD bet drive FY2027–28?
byline: Anchored on FY2026 annual results (52 weeks to 28 March 2026, released 5 June 2026) and the NKD acquisition (closing 31 March 2026, consolidating from FY2027). FY2026 is the reported actual; FY2027 = the 2026/27 year. Forward figures are analyst estimates (e).
footer: Mr Price Group (MRP) — FY2027/28 Scenario Outlook — internal research

blocks.label: House view.
blocks.body: Mr Price is two stories bolted together. The SA base is a high-quality, cash-generative value retailer (FY2026 operating profit +4.3% to R6.0bn; 89.4% cash sales; self-funded store growth) whose EARNINGS path points to modest, decelerating growth — in-line to slight upgrades, not downgrades. The swing factor is NKD: the ~R9.66bn European acquisition that consolidates from FY2027 doubles the footprint but ends the net-cash balance sheet, adds euro translation risk and integration risk, and is the reason statutory FY2026 HEPS grew only ~2% versus ~8% normalised. The risk is therefore to the RATING and to execution, not the SA numbers: the market de-rated the shares 13.7% on the deal. Neutral case is mid-single-digit normalised HEPS growth with NKD roughly neutral in year one; the Bull case needs an SA consumer/rate tailwind plus a clean NKD turnaround; the Bear case is a loss-making NKD with impairment risk on top of a soft SA consumer.

blocks.text: Bottom line: across FY2027–FY2028 the Neutral case is high-single-digit normalised diluted-HEPS growth with market share held in SA; the Bull case requires NKD to turn profitable early and the SA consumer to recover; the Bear case is an NKD integration overrun (and possible goodwill impairment) compounded by a sticky-inflation SA consumer and a heavier acquisition-debt interest bill. The decisive divergence is FY2028, once the NKD outcome is visible.

sections.heading: 2. Where Mr Price stands today (sacred actuals)

blocks.text: Reported FY2026 figures are taken verbatim from the cited results; FY2025 comparatives marked (e) are derived from the reported percentage changes for context. Group revenue was R42.7bn; retail sales R41.1bn. Statutory diluted-HEPS growth (+2.4%) is depressed by once-off NKD transaction costs — management's normalised diluted HEPS grew +8.0% to ~1,489c.

NKD context (from FY2027): up to €487m (~R9.66bn) for a German-headquartered value retailer with 2,000+ stores across seven countries. Combined group revenue is guided toward ~R53bn once consolidated; the deal more than doubles the footprint to >5,000 stores and is the first material offshore operating business — the source of the new euro translation exposure and gearing.

Method: a positive/negative lexicon was counted across the captured text of Mr Price's FY2026 results commentary and the NKD deal/market coverage, then cross-read against the analyst framing in that coverage. The token count is a directional signal, not a precise score — and the counts are run over short-form extracts (the full SENS/AFS binary is not yet archived locally), so absolute numbers are low; the contrast between sources is the signal.

The signal: Mr Price's OWN results language skews mildly positive (~1.3:1) — 'value', 'strength', 'agility', 'confident', 'operating leverage', 'conservative' — but with a visible caution cluster ('challenging', 'soft', 'dampened', 'diluted', 'lower'). The EXTERNAL/market voice around NKD is net negative (~0.7:1) — 'controversial', 'concern', 'rattled', 'fell', 'integration risk'. That split — franchise-positive, strategy-cautious — is the whole story: the SA business is described confidently, the offshore bet is described warily.

rows: 'agility of our operating model… strength of our value retailing DNA… operating leverage in a challenging retail environment… confident… perform across economic cycles' (CEO, FY2026)
SA franchise EARNINGS resilience — in-line to slight upgrade bias
operating profit +4.3%; cash sales 89.4%; 196 new stores; credit book 'managed conservatively' (FY2026)
Market position HELD; self-funded, low-risk growth
'earnings dampened' by once-off NKD transaction costs; statutory HEPS +2% vs normalised +8% (FY2026)
'soft' discretionary demand; tough two-pot-boosted prior-year base; other revenue −1.9% (FY2026)
'integration risk'; first material offshore business; large EUR translation exposure; ends net-cash balance sheet (NKD)
Execution + FX RISK; balance sheet geared up
>2,000 stores; 'more than doubles' footprint; combined revenue toward ~R53bn; value model 'aligned' (NKD)
Medium-term GROWTH optionality IF the turnaround works

blocks.text: Net read: the company's own disclosures still skew positive on the SA franchise (operating leverage, cash generation, store growth, share) while flagging a soft consumer; the bearish voice is the external/market one and is aimed at the NKD bet and the rating. Translating to the three asks — earnings: improving but decelerating in SA, with FY2027 reported growth hostage to NKD's first-year P&L (in-line, slight-upgrade bias on normalised numbers); market share: held/edging up in SA value retail; rating: downgrade-biased until NKD shows it can turn profitable without impairment.
Scenarios are framed on the SA consumer cycle and the NKD turnaround/EUR path rather than a single macro shock. Driver levels are analyst assumptions (e).

rows: Rate-cut cycle resumes; real income recovers; discretionary spend turns up
Constrained but stable; low-single-digit real income
Sticky inflation + fuel; rates high; income squeezed
Late-May 2026 25bp hike is the peak; on hold; CPI ~4.5%
Stable-to-soft EUR aids cost base; clean translation
Rand weakness + EUR volatility; adverse translation
Ahead of plan; turns profitable early; synergies land
Overruns; NKD loss-making; goodwill impairment risk
Gearing sticky; payout trimmed during integration

sections.heading: 5. Projections — FY2027E and FY2028E by scenario

blocks.text: All forward figures are analyst estimates (e), built off the FY2026 sacred anchors and a normalised FY2026 diluted-HEPS base of ~1,489c (the clean base once once-off NKD costs drop out). FY2027 is the first NKD consolidation year — group revenue therefore steps up ~30% on consolidation, which is NOT organic growth; NKD is revenue-heavy but earnings-light in year one. Diluted-HEPS growth is shown versus the FY2026 normalised base.

blocks.label: Reading the spread.
blocks.body: FY2027 is the noisy consolidation year: group revenue jumps ~30% purely because NKD comes on-book, while reported earnings are weighed by NKD's thin first-year margins and the acquisition-debt interest bill — so the headline revenue surge overstates the underlying improvement. The decisive divergence is FY2028: once the NKD outcome is visible, the Bull-to-Bear normalised-DHEPS gap widens to ~515 cents (1,902 vs 1,387, ~37%), driven almost entirely by whether NKD turns profitable and by the EUR/ZAR path. That is where the re-rating-or-de-rating debate gets settled — far more than by the SA base.

sections.heading: 6. What flips the rating — upgrade vs downgrade triggers

items: NKD reaching operating breakeven/profit ahead of guidance; explicit synergy, margin and ROCE-hurdle targets disclosed; SA comparable sales re-accelerating above RSP inflation (positive volumes); an EUR/ZAR translation tailwind; and the ~63% payout held — confirming the balance sheet can carry the deal.

NKD loss-making with integration overruns or any goodwill impairment; SA volumes turning negative as discretionary demand stays soft; the acquisition-debt interest bill running heavier than modelled; the dividend trimmed to fund gearing; and the statutory-vs-normalised earnings gap persisting into FY2027.

FY2026 statutory diluted HEPS grew only +2.4% versus +8.0% normalised — the gap is the once-off NKD transaction costs. FY2027 is the first clean read; if normalised growth still lags because NKD operating losses plus interest simply replace the one-offs, the 'real' improvement is weaker than the headline and supports the de-rating thesis.
NKD funding: what is the exact debt/cash mix for the ~R9.66bn consideration, the pro-forma net debt/EBITDA, and the expected FY2027 interest charge?
NKD economics: what ROCE and margin does NKD earn today, what is the breakeven timeline and the synergy/margin-uplift target, and how is goodwill-impairment risk being framed?
FX policy: how will euro translation be managed and disclosed (segmental reporting, net-investment hedging), and what EUR/ZAR assumption underpins guidance?
SA base: what selling-price-inflation and volume assumptions sit behind FY2027, given soft discretionary demand and the tough two-pot-boosted prior-year comparatives?
Capital returns: does the ~63% payout policy hold through the NKD integration, or should we expect a lower payout while gearing is worked down?
